CUET subjects can unlock.

Enter your CUET marks subject by subject. We compute your DU merit the way the university does and show only programmes you are actually eligible for.

How DU scores you — read this first

Each CUET subject is marked out of 250. Delhi University does not use one single score for every course:

• B.A., B.Com and a few science courses (like B.Sc Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science) count your best language + best 3 domain subjects = out of 1000.

• Most B.Sc science courses (Physics, Chemistry, Zoology, Applied/Physical Science and similar) count only your best 3 domain subjects, with no language = out of 750.

So we show you both scores and compare each college against the correct one automatically. Enter every subject you appeared in.

Science programmes need science subjects. A B.Sc in Physics, Chemistry, Maths or the life sciences requires you to have appeared in the matching CUET subject. If you did not take it, that programme cannot be offered to you and we leave it out, however high your score. (The 750 vs 1000 split here is about science vs general courses, not about B.Tech, which is not in this list.)

Language (best one) — out of 250
Domain subjects — out of 250 each (add every subject you appeared in)
General Test (GAT) — out of 250 (optional)
General merit · with language
Science merit · best 3, no language

Check your admission probability for one college and programme.

For science B.Sc courses this is out of 750; for others out of 1000.

Gaurav

Founder, Counselling Dost

Most of you wanted to know a little more about me, so here it is.

My name is Gaurav. I am originally from Madhubani, Bihar, and was born and raised in a lower middle-class family.

Academically, I scored 90.6% in Class 10 and 95% in Class 12. After school, I received admission offers from Hansraj College for B.Sc. Mathematics and a few other Delhi University colleges for B.Sc. Computer Science. However, due to financial constraints, moving to Delhi was not possible at that time.

I decided to take a drop year and prepare for JEE. Recently, I completed my B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Tezpur University, Assam, a GFTI and Central University.

I have always been deeply interested in college admissions and counselling. During my JEE preparation and college research phase, I spent countless hours understanding colleges, cutoffs, branches, and counselling processes. This interest eventually helped me secure an internship at a counselling startup, where I gained practical experience guiding students.

Since my second year of college, I have been actively helping students with counselling and admissions. Over the years, I have guided more than 1,000 students across various counselling processes, including JEE, JoSAA, CSAB, CUET, IPU, UPTAC, and others.

I started this page around the time I was graduating and choosing between multiple job offers. My goal is simple: to use my engineering and technology background to make college counselling more logical, transparent, and data-driven through analytics, historical trends, and AI-powered tools.

I do not charge students for general queries, and I intend to keep it that way. I am doing this because I genuinely enjoy helping students make better decisions, not because I want to make money from counselling. I am already placed and will be moving to Delhi this August for work.

Thank you to everyone who follows, supports, and trusts this page. I hope the content and tools I build continue to help students make informed choices.

If you'd like to connect with me personally, my Instagram ID is: @iota.gaurav
